audience: Business owners, marketers, web developers and SMEs auditing a new or existing website for search engines.
primaryDutchSearchIntent: KMO's en websiteverantwoordelijken een uitvoerbare controlelijst geven voor crawlbaarheid, indexering, URL's, mobiel, structured data, performance en monitoring.

searchIntent.question: How should an SME audit crawlability, indexing, canonicals, redirects, mobile rendering, structured data, performance and monitoring?
searchIntent.geographicContext: English-speaking SMEs, with VisualVibe services based in Limburg, Belgium.

directAnswer: A technical SEO audit first verifies that important URLs return usable pages, can be crawled and indexed as intended, and have consistent canonicals and redirects. It then checks internal discovery, mobile rendering, structured data, performance, accessibility, launch controls and ongoing monitoring.
title: Technical SEO checklist for SME websites
metaDescription: Auditing technical SEO? Check crawlability, indexing, canonicals, redirects, mobile usability, structured data, Core Web Vitals and monitoring.
h1: Technical SEO checklist for SME websites

internalLinks.anchor: local SEO for SMEs in Limburg
get found in AI search results
why a fast website ranks better

factsToPreserve: Meeting Google's technical requirements does not guarantee indexing or rankings.
robots.txt controls crawling but is not a reliable method for removing a URL from search results.
A sitemap supports discovery but does not guarantee indexing.
Canonical annotations are signals, not absolute directives.
Redirect behaviour and destination pages must be tested after migration.
Structured data must match visible page content and does not guarantee rich results.
Core Web Vitals and tool scores require context and do not guarantee rankings.
Technical SEO cannot compensate for weak content, unclear intent or low credibility.
